Home / Art Gallery / Botanical Abstraction
Collection 03

Botanical Abstraction

“Abstraction begins where intuition takes the place of certainty.”

The Botanical Abstraction series by contemporary artist Nona Dronova explores the hidden emotional structure of flowers through colour, texture and layered painting. Inspired by botanical forms yet liberated from direct representation, these acrylic panels transform nature into expressive abstract compositions.

For Nona Dronova, creating an abstract painting is a meditative process. Layer after layer, colour interacts with colour, texture emerges from texture, and unexpected visual relationships gradually appear across the surface. Rather than controlling every detail, the artist allows the painting to evolve organically, discovering new forms, rhythms and luminous effects as the work unfolds.

The Power of the Rose series reflects this philosophy, where botanical inspiration becomes a language of colour, movement and contemplation. Each panel records not the appearance of a flower, but the emotional energy and inner harmony that nature reveals through the creative process.

“True abstraction cannot be planned. It is discovered layer by layer, until the painting begins to reveal something even the artist could not foresee.”
